SEARCH
Package com.ragcore.service.vector

Class InMemoryVectorStore

java.lang.Object
com.ragcore.service.vector.InMemoryVectorStore
All Implemented Interfaces:
VectorStore
@Service public class InMemoryVectorStore extends Object implements VectorStore
In-memory implementation of VectorStore.

Chunks are stored in a CopyOnWriteArrayList, which is thread-safe and allows concurrent uploads without data corruption. Similarity search uses cosine similarity computed manually — no external math library is used.

All data is lost on application restart. This implementation is intended for development and course use; a persistent store would be needed for production.

  • Constructor Details

    • InMemoryVectorStore

      @Autowired public InMemoryVectorStore(EmbeddingService embeddingService)

  • Method Details

    • store

      public void store(List<Chunk> chunks)
      Stores a list of chunks into the vector store.

      Each chunk will be embedded (if not already) and stored for later retrieval via similarity search. Implementations should use the EmbeddingService to generate embeddings for chunks that do not already have one.

      Any chunk that does not already carry an embedding will be embedded via EmbeddingService.embed(java.lang.String) before being added to the store.

      Specified by:
      store in interface VectorStore
      Parameters:
      chunks - the list of chunks to store; must not be null

    • search

      public List<Chunk> search(String query, int topK)
      Searches the vector store for the top-K most relevant chunks to the given query string.

      The query is first converted to an embedding vector, then compared against all stored chunk embeddings using cosine similarity. Results are returned in descending order of similarity.

      The query string is embedded and then compared against every stored chunk using cosine similarity. Results are returned in descending order of similarity.

      Specified by:
      search in interface VectorStore
      Parameters:
      query - the search query text; must not be null or empty
      topK - the maximum number of results to return; must be positive
      Returns:
      a list of the most similar chunks, ordered by descending similarity

    • cosineSimilarity

      public double cosineSimilarity(float[] a, float[] b)
      Computes the cosine similarity between two float vectors.

      Formula: cos(θ) = (A · B) / (|A| * |B|)

      Returns values in [-1, 1]: 1 means identical direction (most similar), 0 means perpendicular (unrelated), -1 means opposite direction.

      Uses double accumulation to avoid rounding errors over high-dimensional vectors (e.g. 1536 dimensions from OpenAI). Returns 0.0 for zero vectors to prevent division by zero.

      Parameters:
      a - first vector
      b - second vector
      Returns:
      cosine similarity in [-1, 1]
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if the vectors have different lengths

    • clear

      public void clear()
      Clears all stored chunks from the vector store.
      Specified by:
      clear in interface VectorStore

    • size

      public int size()
      Returns the number of chunks currently held in the store.
      Returns:
      chunk count
